title: Chytroma amara
taxonomy: Chytroma amara is classified as a species of fungus in the genus Chytroma, family Chytriomycetaceae, order Chytridiales, class Chytridiomycetes, and phylum Chytridiomycota. This classification is based on its characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. Chytroma amara is a member of the group of organisms commonly known as chytrids, which are a type of aquatic fungus. Chytridiomycota is a phylum of fungi that are primarily aquatic and saprotrophic, meaning they obtain their nutrients by decomposing organic matter. The classification of Chytroma amara provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.
